title: Cheilosoria belangeri
taxonomy: Cheilosoria belangeri is classified as a species of fern in the family Hemionitidaceae. It belongs to the genus Cheilosoria and is native to tropical Asia. The genus Cheilosoria is a group of epiphytic ferns that are typically found in humid, shaded environments. Cheilosoria belangeri is a type of lip fern, characterized by the presence of lip-like structures on its leaves. It is a relatively rare and understudied species, with limited information available about its classification and characteristics.

intro: Cheilosoria belangeri, also known as the Indian lip fern, is a rare and endangered species of fern native to the Western Ghats of India. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues.
